notes: Realistic mixed-severity case: 8 rows producing 2 CRITICAL + 5 HIGH = 7 findings. All six rules fire at least once. svc_batch triggers both R1 (SQL_LOGIN Admin, can't enforce MFA) and R6 (generic service-account name). dead_dba triggers R2 (WINDOWS_LOGIN Admin with no last_active and create_date >90 days old). cross_dev triggers R3 (same principal holds Admin in both appdb_dev and appdb_prod). ghost_login triggers R4 (no DB user mapping). frank_explicit triggers R5 (explicit WRITE grant outside any role). admin triggers a second R6 (generic admin name). alice_readonly is intentionally clean (no rule fires). Plan called for 1 MEDIUM severity finding but no R1-R6 rule emits MEDIUM severity; the rules engine only supports CRITICAL and HIGH. Actual mix is 2 CRITICAL + 5 HIGH (7 total) which fully tests narrator clustering and theme-grouping across severity tiers.
